title: Equality: what it means and why it matters [Book Review]
author: McManus

opening_move.description: The review opens with a polemical diagnosis of contemporary democratic crisis — the 2024 US election as symptom of entrenched oligarchy — to frame the reviewed book's stakes.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Understanding what equality means in an era of intensifying plutocracy and meritocratic ideology

theoretical_positioning.description: The review endorses the Piketty-Sandel dialogue but presses for a more radical structural critique that goes beyond the conversation's reformism.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading of the Piketty-Sandel dialogue, with political-diagnostic framing
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review with strongly polemical political framing. The reviewer's own normative commitments (socialist egalitarianism) shape the critical assessment.

case_type.case_name: 2024 US Presidential election; Piketty & Sandel, Equality (2025)

confidence_notes: Short book review (3 pages) with unusually strong political framing. Reviewer is Matthew McManus — same person whose book was reviewed in 00756-x — lending the review a distinctive ideological voice.
